enonce.texte:
📝 Exercise 1: Match the community development terms on the left with their correct definitions on the right.

Column A (Terms)

  • 1. Community development
  • 2. Infrastructure
  • 3. Clean-up campaign
  • 4. Fundraising
  • 5. Awareness
  • 6. Volunteer

Column B (Definitions)

  • A. The activity of collecting money for a cause
  • B. A person who offers their services freely
  • C. The process of improving a community's well-being
  • D. Knowledge or perception of a situation
  • E. Basic physical structures needed for a society to function
  • F. An organized activity to remove litter and waste

questions.texte: Match each term in Column A with its correct definition in Column B.

sousQuestions.texte: Community development
sousQuestions.reponse: C. The process of improving a community's well-being

sousQuestions.texte: Infrastructure
sousQuestions.reponse: E. Basic physical structures needed for a society to function

sousQuestions.texte: Clean-up campaign
sousQuestions.reponse: F. An organized activity to remove litter and waste

sousQuestions.texte: Fundraising
sousQuestions.reponse: A. The activity of collecting money for a cause

sousQuestions.texte: Awareness
sousQuestions.reponse: D. Knowledge or perception of a situation

sousQuestions.texte: Volunteer
sousQuestions.reponse: B. A person who offers their services freely

enonce.texte:
📖 Exercise 2: Read the text about community development in a Cameroonian village and answer the questions.

"In the village of Nkongsamba, the community decided to work together to improve their living conditions. The traditional chief, along with the youth association, organized a series of development actions. First, they launched a clean-up campaign to remove waste from the streets. Then, they started fundraising to build a new health centre. Many villagers volunteered their time and skills. Some helped with construction, while others raised awareness about hygiene and sanitation. The women's group also organized training on small business management. Today, the village has better infrastructure, cleaner surroundings, and a stronger sense of community."
images: A traditional village in Cameroon with community members working together on a development project, cheerful and collaborative atmosphere, colorful African village setting

questions.texte: Answer the following questions based on the text.

sousQuestions.texte: What were the two main activities organized by the community?
sousQuestions.reponse: They organized a clean-up campaign and started fundraising to build a new health centre.

sousQuestions.texte: Who volunteered to help with the construction?
sousQuestions.reponse: Many villagers volunteered their time and skills to help with construction.

sousQuestions.texte: What did the women's group organize?
sousQuestions.reponse: The women's group organized training on small business management.

sousQuestions.texte: What are the three positive results mentioned at the end of the text?
sousQuestions.reponse: The village has better infrastructure, cleaner surroundings, and a stronger sense of community.

enonce.texte:
✍️ Exercise 3: Complete the sentences with the appropriate community development vocabulary from the box below.

Vocabulary box: clean-up, fundraising, awareness, development, infrastructure, volunteer, community

1. The _____________ campaign collected enough money to build a new school.

2. We need to raise _____________ about the importance of recycling.

3. The government is investing in new _____________ such as roads and bridges.

4. Many people _____________ their time to help those in need.

5. The _____________ came together to solve the water shortage problem.

6. The annual _____________ event removed tons of waste from the river.

7. Sustainable _____________ requires the participation of all citizens.

questions.texte: Complete each sentence with the correct word from the vocabulary box.
questions.reponse: 1. fundraising, 2. awareness, 3. infrastructure, 4. volunteer, 5. community, 6. clean-up, 7. development

sousQuestions.texte: Sentence 1: The _____________ campaign collected enough money to build a new school.
sousQuestions.reponse: fundraising

sousQuestions.texte: Sentence 2: We need to raise _____________ about the importance of recycling.
sousQuestions.reponse: awareness

sousQuestions.texte: Sentence 3: The government is investing in new _____________ such as roads and bridges.
sousQuestions.reponse: infrastructure

sousQuestions.texte: Sentence 4: Many people _____________ their time to help those in need.
sousQuestions.reponse: volunteer

sousQuestions.texte: Sentence 5: The _____________ came together to solve the water shortage problem.
sousQuestions.reponse: community

sousQuestions.texte: Sentence 6: The annual _____________ event removed tons of waste from the river.
sousQuestions.reponse: clean-up

sousQuestions.texte: Sentence 7: Sustainable _____________ requires the participation of all citizens.
sousQuestions.reponse: development

enonce.texte:
💡 Exercise 4: Read the situation below and answer the questions using the community development vocabulary.

Situation: Your community in Bafoussam has a problem: there is no clean drinking water. Many people get sick from waterborne diseases. The community leaders want to find a solution. They have a meeting to discuss possible actions.
images: A meeting of community members in Bafoussam, Cameroon, discussing a water project, diverse group of people, traditional and modern clothing, African village setting

questions.texte: Answer the following questions to develop a community project proposal.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify the problem in the community. (Identify)
sousQuestions.reponse: The problem is the lack of clean drinking water, which causes waterborne diseases.

sousQuestions.texte: Propose two actions the community could take to solve this problem. (Propose)
sousQuestions.reponse: Possible actions: 1) Organize fundraising to build a well or water treatment system. 2) Launch an awareness campaign about water purification methods.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain who would be involved in these actions and why their participation is important. (Explain)
sousQuestions.reponse: The community members would be involved through volunteering. Local authorities would provide support. Health workers could educate people about hygiene. Women's groups could organize training. Everyone's participation is important because community development requires collective effort and solidarity.

sousQuestions.texte: Justify why this project is important for the community development. (Justify)
sousQuestions.reponse: This project is important because clean water is essential for health, education, and economic activities. It reduces diseases, improves school attendance, and allows people to work productively. It is the foundation of sustainable community development.

enonce.texte:
🔍 Exercise 5: Read the following scenarios and evaluate the community development actions described.

Scenario A: A group of young people in Garoua decided to organize a community garden. They worked together to plant vegetables. They shared the harvest with vulnerable families. They also trained younger children on how to grow food.

Scenario B: In Kribi, the local authorities received funding to build a new market. However, they did not consult the community. They hired external workers instead of using local labor. The market was built but many local traders could not afford the new stalls.
images: A community garden in Garoua, Cameroon with young people working together, lively and positive atmosphere, African garden setting
A new but empty market in Kribi, Cameroon with local traders looking disappointed, contrast between modern building and unhappy people

questions.texte: Analyze both scenarios and answer the questions.

sousQuestions.texte: Analyze Scenario A: What are the positive aspects of this community action? (Analyze)
sousQuestions.reponse: Positive aspects: 1) It addresses food security. 2) It involves community members actively. 3) It shares benefits with vulnerable groups. 4) It educates younger generation. 5) It promotes sustainability and self-reliance.

sousQuestions.texte: Evaluate Scenario B: What mistakes were made in this development project? (Evaluate)
sousQuestions.reponse: Mistakes made: 1) No community consultation. 2) Did not involve local labor, missing opportunity for empowerment. 3) Did not consider affordability for local traders. 4) Did not address the real needs of the community. 5) Created economic exclusion instead of inclusion.

sousQuestions.texte: Create a proposal to improve Scenario B. What would you do differently? (Create)
sousQuestions.reponse: I would: 1) Organize community meetings to identify their needs. 2) Involve local labor in construction, creating jobs. 3) Design stalls at affordable prices. 4) Include local traders in the planning. 5) Ensure transparency and accountability in fund use. 6) Develop a maintenance plan involving the community.
